calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend Following Demurrer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City Of Daly City's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend Following Demurrer.

Defendant City of Daly City's unopposed Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend Following Demurrer is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(f)(2). Section 581(f)(2) allows a court, upon motion, to dismiss an action when, after a demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend, the plaintiff fails to amend it within the time allowed by the court. (See Leader v Health Industries of America, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 603.) Here, the court sustained a demurrer to the entire complaint in February 2025 and, nine months later, Plaintiff has yet to amend.

Good cause appearing, the court dismisses the action in its entirety and orders that judgment may be entered in favor of Moving Defendant.

The court denies the motion to vacate the OSC. Such a request must be addressed to the department that issued the OSC.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

(see word minutes for full details)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2025 Continued To Feb-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Robert Mitchell. Esq. 650-365-7715 Ridley Master for Defendant. (in-person)
No other Appearance.
Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR#11975, 99-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, reported.

ARGUED. Mr. Mitchell contests the tentative ruling and argues. The Court review proof of delivery of courtesy copies provided by Mr. Mitchell. The Court Court suggest to bring in courtesy copies at the time of the hearing to save time. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY OF DALY CITY's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling. The matter is continued to 2/10/25 conditioned on all courtesy copies of papers to be provided to Dept. 302 by 4pm on Monday 2/3/25. Mr. Mitchell to submit a proposed order to the contestdept302tr@sftc.org ASAP and serve the order on the other side.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR#11975, 99-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, reported.(302/JMQ)
